subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: A sanitary drainage system must be in accordance with AS/NZS 3500.2 with the following variations:
(a) Clause 4.3.1 insert subclause: (i) Shall not have DN40 or DN50 traps installed.
(b) Clause 4.4.1 insert the following as the second paragraph: Boundary trap or inspection shafts shall not terminate within:
(i) Bedrooms
(ii) Living rooms
(iii) Lounge rooms
(iv) Music rooms
(v) Television rooms
(vi) Kitchens
(vii) Dining rooms
(viii) Sewing rooms
(ix) Study
(x) Playroom
(xi) Family room
(xii) Home theatre room
(xiii) Sunroom
(xiv) Bathroom
(xv) Laundry
(xvi) Water closet
(xvii) Pantry
(xviii) Walk-in wardrobe
(xix) Corridor
(xx) Hallway
(xxi) Lobby
(xxii) Photographic darkroom
(xxii) Clothes drying room; and
(xxiv) Other spaces of a specialised nature occupied neither frequently nor for extended periods.

(c) Clause 4.5.2 insert subclause: (c) Soil and waste stacks shall not discharge through a reflux valve except where a reflux valve is installed at the connection to the sewer required with surcharging sewers or where a Network Utility Operator may prescribe soffit requirements.
NOTE: Where the Network Utility Operator prescribes a reflux valves to be installed for Soffit Requirements, the Network Utility Operator is responsible for the regulation of the reflux valve.

(d) Clause 4.5.3 delete and insert: Where a surcharge is likely to occur a reflux valve shall be installed immediately downstream of the boundary trap or inspection shaft.The invert of the outlet of the reflux valve shall be installed a minimum of 80mm higher than the invert of the Network Utility Operator’s system it is connected to.
NOTE 1: See NSW Figures C2D4a and C2D4b.
NOTE 2: Where a reflux valve is installed the valve remains the responsibility of the property owner.
(e) Clause 4.6.2 insert subclause: (d) be installed vertically with no offsets.

(f) Clause 4.6.7.1 insert the following as the second paragraph: A submerged floor waste gully and compact floor waste gully with water seal trap insert shall only be used in sanitary plumbing systems.

(g) Clause 4.7.1 insert subclauses (i) and (j): (i) At each drain connecting one or more water closets or slop hoppers to provide direct maintenance rodding access and be raised to finished surface level in an accessible location with a sealed airtight cover. If access to an under floor area is more than 600mm above the inspection opening, the riser may be omitted. (j) at the point to which a drain connects to an onsite wastewater treatment system, wet well or similar apparatuses. For wet wells and similar apparatuses with electrical components located within, a junction of 45° in the opposite direction of flow and vertical riser terminated at ground level with a sealed airtight cover shall be installed to direct future drain maintenance away from the wet well or apparatus.
NOTE: Water Closets and Slop Hoppers where connected to sanitary plumbing and above ground pipework using drainage principles are not subject to the requirements of Clause 4.7.1 (i).
(h) Insert Clause 4.12 Flexible joints in drains where:
(i) 4.12.1 - General: Flexible joints should be installed to accommodate movement to drains that may otherwise cause damage and affect the normal performance of a sanitary drainage system.
NOTE: See PCA Variation Clause 5.6 for information on Drains in other than stable ground.

(ii) 4.12.2 – Location: Where flexible joints are installed in a sanitary drainage system to accommodate ground movement, a detailed design of the installation shall be prepared by a suitably qualified engineer or recognised expert for the sanitary drainage system to avoid damage from ground movement. The design shall include the proposed locations of the flexible joints and any alternative methods of support for the sanitary drainage system. The flexible joints shall be installed in the location and configuration detailed in the design and a copy of the design showing the location of the flexible joints shall be provided to the local plumbing regulator prior to an inspection.
NOTE 1: See Appendix G for information on the installation of plastic pipe drainage systems in unstable soils within Australia.
NOTE 2: AS2870 provides special design considerations for drains associated with residential slab or footing systems on moderately, highly or extremely reactive soils
NOTE 3: See PCA NSW Variation Clause 5.6 for information on Drains in other than stable ground.

(i) Clause 5.6 delete and insert: If drains are to be laid in filled, unstable, water-charged ground or ground prone to movement, methods of support, bedding or accommodating movement, shall be designed to withstand and suit the ground conditions where sanitary drainage on sites with soils classified H1, H2 and E should accommodate ground movement.

NOTE 1: For proclaimed mine subsidence or landslip district the appropriate authority should be referred to for advice on subsidence or landslip design condition parameters for proposed drainage systems.
NOTE 2: Sites with soils classified M or P may be provided with protection from ground movement.
NOTE 3: Clause 3.8.3 provide special design considerations for drains in close proximity to footings.
NOTE 4: See PCA Variation Clause 4.12 for Flexible joints in drains requirements.
NOTE 5: See Appendix G for information on the installation of plastic pipe drainage systems in unstable soils within Australia.
NOTE 6: AS2870 provides special design considerations for drains associated with residential slab or footing systems on moderately, highly or extremely reactive soils.

NOTE 7: In Australia performance requirements are prescribed in the Plumbing Code of Australia to ensure that a design may be suitable. A performance solution is to be provided to the Plumbing Regulator.
(j) Clause 12.6.3 delete and insert: The wet well shall have a minimum DN 80 Vent except where:
(i) manufactured wet wells are watermark approved with a smaller vent size; or
(ii) the pump and wet well is approved for use by the Network Utility Operator of the pressure sewer system to which it will connect and has a self venting lid or similar.
(iii) Venting of a wet well is not to cause nuisance to occupants of a dwelling.

(k) Clause 12.7 delete and insert: : The pump discharge pressure piping shall conform to the relevant sections of AS/NZS 3500.1 and this document. The outlet pipe shall discharge to one of the following locations:
(i) An inspection chamber
(ii) A boundary trap shaft
(iii) A stack below the lowest fixture connection on any floor.
(iv) A drain or combined discharge pipe, provided the connection is at least 2.5m form any other connection.
(v) Downstream of a reflux valve or at least 2.5m upstream of a reflux valve.
(vi) A minimum of 1m downstream of a boundary trap.
(vii) Direct to the Network Utility Operator’s sewer where approved by the Network Utility Operator.
(viii) A marker tape must be laid along the top of all pump discharge or rising mains pipes at intervals of not more than 3 m.
NOTE 1: Sewage management facilities shall be accredited by NSW Health and comply with local government requirements.

NOTE 2: Before a connection is made to pump raw sewage or effluent from a septic tank or holding well to the Network Utility Operator’s sewer, an application must be made to that Network Utility Operator. Applicants must meet the pump to sewer requirements and conditions for all connections to the sewer.

callout.p: Where a reflux valve is installed the valve remains the responsibility of the property owner.
